Title: Successful Login
Preconditions: User exists in the system with valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in with valid credentials.
2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager returns true indicating a successful login.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully.

Title: Failed Login - Invalid Username
Preconditions: User does not exist in the system.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in with invalid username.
2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager returns false indicating a failed login.
Expected Result: User is not logged in due to invalid username.

Title: Failed Login - Invalid Password
Preconditions: User exists in the system but with incorrect password.
Test Steps:
1. User attempts to log in with correct username and incorrect password.
2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager returns false indicating a failed login.
Expected Result: User is not logged in due to incorrect password.

Title: Create API
Preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to create APIs.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. User requests creation of a new API.
3. APIManager receives the request.
4. APIManager creates the new API with specified name and version.
Expected Result: New API is created successfully.

Title: Delete API
Preconditions: User is logged in and has permission to delete APIs.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. User requests deletion of an existing API.
3. APIManager receives the request.
4. APIManager deletes the specified API.
Expected Result: Existing API is deleted successfully.

Title: Handle Request
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager sends a request to the Service.
3. Service receives the request.
4. Service processes the request.
Expected Result: Service successfully handles the request.

Title: Generate Report
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager generates a report.
3. Report receives the request to generate a report.
4. Report generates the specified report.
Expected Result: Report is generated successfully.

Title: Manage Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager interacts with the DataManager.
3. DataManager manages the specified data.
4. APIManager receives confirmation from DataManager.
Expected Result: Data is managed successfully.

Title: Full Interaction Flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and all components are operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager sends a request to the Service.
5. Service processes the request.
6. APIManager generates a report.
7. Report generates the specified report.
8. APIManager interacts with the DataManager.
9. DataManager manages the specified data.
10. APIManager confirms data management with Main.
Expected Result: All components interact successfully following the full interaction flow.

Title: Logout
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. User requests to log out.
3. APIManager receives the logout request.
4. APIManager logs out the user.
Expected Result: User is logged out successfully.

Title: Failed API Creation - Duplicate Name
Preconditions: User is logged in and an API with the same name already exists.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. User requests creation of an API with an existing name.
3. APIManager receives the request.
4. APIManager checks for duplicate API name.
5. APIManager returns an error indicating the API name is already in use.
Expected Result: API creation fails due to duplicate name.

Title: Failed API Deletion - Nonexistent API
Preconditions: User is logged in and the specified API does not exist.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. User requests deletion of a non-existent API.
3. APIManager receives the request.
4. APIManager checks for the existence of the API.
5. APIManager returns an error indicating the API does not exist.
Expected Result: API deletion fails due to non-existent API.

Title: Failed Request Handling - Invalid Request
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager sends an invalid request to the Service.
3. Service receives the invalid request.
4. Service returns an error indicating the request is invalid.
Expected Result: Service fails to handle the invalid request.

Title: Failed Report Generation - Invalid Name
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager generates a report with an invalid name.
3. Report receives the request to generate an invalid report.
4. Report returns an error indicating the report name is invalid.
Expected Result: Report generation fails due to invalid name.

Title: Failed Data Management - Invalid Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and APIManager is operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager interacts with the DataManager with invalid data.
3. DataManager returns an error indicating the data is invalid.
4. APIManager receives the error from DataManager.
Expected Result: Data management fails due to invalid data.

Title: Error Handling in Full Interaction Flow
Preconditions: User is logged in and all components are operational.
Test Steps:
1. User logs in successfully.
2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager sends an invalid request to the Service.
5. Service returns an error indicating the request is invalid.
6. APIManager generates a report with an invalid name.
7. Report returns an error indicating the report name is invalid.
8. APIManager interacts with the DataManager with invalid data.
9. DataManager returns an error indicating the data is invalid.
10. APIManager confirms errors with Main.
Expected Result: All components handle errors appropriately during the full interaction flow.

2. APIManager receives the login request.
3. APIManager verifies the user's credentials.
4. APIManager returns false indicating a failed login.
